description: You are a Systems Administrator investigating a production server that appears to be showing '95% memory utilization' in basic monitoring tools. You know this is standard Linux behavior (disk caching) and need to build a 'True Memory Accounting' dashboard that clearly separates actual application memory from cache/buffers and shows the true utilization percentage. Create and save a Graphite dashboard named 'Linux Memory Accounting' using live memory metrics from the collectd namespace (not the servers namespace). The dashboard must include a graph titled 'Absolute Memory Breakdown' showing a stacked area view of all collectd.*.memory.* metrics using stacked() and aliasByNode() on the third node index; a graph titled 'True Application Memory Percent' using asPercent() to show collectd.*.memory.memory-used as a percentage of the sumSeries
